w:r.w:t: Compiled from 7 Source Documents including Claude Intelligence Reports, Manus AI Technical Audit, YC Hackathon Strategy, Founder Destiny Blueprint, and Original Market Research
Zero global prior art on core
K S Akshay Founder and CEO Rashi Technologies
New Horizon College of Engineering, Marathahalli, Bengaluru 560103

DevPulse is the only developer tool in the world that simultaneously performs API security vulnerability scanning and Large Language Model API cost intelligence in one unified workflow. Seven independent documents, including research from Claude, Manus AI, and market analysis tools, all reached the same conclusion: this gap is real, large, unaddressed, and the timing in 2026 is exactly right.

DevPulse is the first developer-native AgentGuard. It sits between the developer's code and the LLM provider. It automatically detects infinite agent loops, isolates hidden thinking token costs, redacts sensitive data before it leaves the network, and delivers actionable security and cost intelligence directly inside VS Code where developers already live. No existing product does all of this. No existing product does any two of these together.
The Three Forces Creating This Window Now

Postman killed its free multi-user tier in March 2026. Over 30,000 public workspaces were found leaking live API keys including Razorpay and New Relic credentials. Valuation dropped 40 percent. Five hundred thousand companies are actively seeking alternatives right now. This switching momentum lasts 12 to 18 months. DevPulse enters as Postman's most painful failure creates the perfect acquisition opportunity.
FORCE 2 AI API COSTS ARE UNMANAGEABLE

A single rogue AI agent stuck in an infinite reasoning loop can spend 10,000 USD over a weekend. Models like OpenAI o3 and Claude Opus generate invisible thinking tokens that multiply costs by 10 to 30 times per call. Seventy-two percent of AI startups report unexpected LLM cost spikes in their first six months. No affordable tool tracks this at the per-feature level. Developers are terrified and nobody is helping them.
FORCE 3 API SECURITY IS MANDATORY BUT UNAFFORDABLE

PCI DSS version 4.0.1 enacted March 2025 mandates continuous API security testing for all payment-processing organisations. Enterprise tools start at 650 USD per month. Salt Security deals start at 200,000 USD annually. SMEs and AI startups, the fastest-growing developer segment, have absolutely nothing in their budget range. DevPulse fills this gap at 29 to 99 USD per month.
What All 7 Source Documents Agreed On
Unified security plus cost category gap is real. Postman timing is perfect. Six missing features are the path forward.
Build Postman parser first, then unified risk score engine
DevPulse is only product with all matrix columns YES. No competitor combines both domains.
File all 4 patents same day. Launch Postman import as viral hook.
Manus AI Technical Audit (v1 and v2)
Current codebase is strong prototype. Python backend, VS Code extension, and core intelligence features need to be built.
Postman Collection Parser and Thinking Token Attribution are keys to the kingdom
DevPulse should become the Nervous System for AI-native companies. GitHub badges and Reasoning Efficiency Score create identity shift.
Open-source Thinking Token Library. Build Compliance-as-Code generator for revenue.
Market data validates astrological alignment. June to July 2026 is optimal launch and funding window.
Target Accel India Atoms AI Cohort and Peak XV Surge immediately
Jupiter transit June 2026 into Cancer is Golden Period for funding and launch. Saturn through Pisces demands disciplined execution.
Public launch or major funding push by June 2026 is optimal timing
YC Hackathon Pitch and Development Roadmap
Three features win the YC hackathon: VS Code extension, Thinking Token isolation, and Autonomous Kill-Switch demo.
Build 3-minute disaster demo: rogue agent detected and killed automatically
Section 2: The Developer World in 2026 — Deep Research

This section combines verified research from developer surveys, industry reports, and direct community data published in 2025 and 2026. Understanding the exact daily reality of the target developer is the foundation for every product decision DevPulse must make.
How Professional Developers Live Their Work Day

VS Code and Visual Studio maintained their position as the most used development environments for the fourth consecutive year in 2026. VS Code is the operating system of the developer working day. Every external tool requiring a developer to leave VS Code is a source of friction that reduces adoption, reduces trust, and eventually gets removed from the workflow.

Seventy-three percent of engineering teams now use AI coding tools every single day, up from 41 percent in 2025 and 18 percent in 2024. The shift happened in one year. Developers are building more, faster, and with more API endpoints than at any point in the history of software development. Forty-one percent of all code written in 2025 is AI-generated. When AI generates 41 percent of code, the security scanning burden becomes humanly impossible to manage manually.
CRITICAL RESEARCH FINDING — DEVELOPER PRODUCTIVITY LAB 2025

Developers spend an average of 3.5 hours per week just managing their toolchain: switching between tools, updating credentials, dealing with integration issues, and learning new interfaces. That is almost 20 percent of a 40-hour work week spent on tools instead of building products. DevPulse eliminates this waste by collapsing multiple tools into one unified workflow inside VS Code.

GitLab's 2025 Global DevSecOps Report found that 49 percent of development teams use more than five AI tools across use cases. Critically, reducing cognitive load was the second highest motivation for consolidating tools. Developers already know they have too many tools. They already feel the pain. They are actively looking for consolidation but they stay because switching cost anxiety keeps them trapped.

One developer wrote publicly in January 2026: I counted the tools my team uses. The number was forty-seven. We consolidated to two tools that did 90 percent of what we needed. Our incident response time dropped from 45 minutes to 12 minutes. That developer is the exact DevPulse target customer. They have already learned the lesson. They are ready.
The Trust Crisis — The Core Opportunity

More developers are using AI tools but their trust is falling. Positive sentiment toward AI tools dropped from above 70 percent in 2023 and 2024 to 60 percent in 2025. Eighty-seven percent are concerned about the accuracy of AI agents. Eighty-one percent have concerns about security and data privacy. Sixty-six percent struggle with AI solutions that are close but ultimately miss the mark.
WHY THIS IS DEVPULSE'S BIGGEST OPPORTUNITY

Developers are being forced to use more AI tools but they trust them less every quarter. They are generating more AI-written code with unknown vulnerabilities. They are spending more on LLM APIs with no visibility into why. The anxiety is real, growing, and completely unaddressed. When DevPulse scans an API and reports this endpoint has a High severity BOLA vulnerability AND is costing you 340 rupees per day in LLM compute, it gives the developer certainty in an environment of total uncertainty. That certainty is the product. Not the features.
The Four Psychological Triggers That Break the Comfort Zone

Based on verified research and the analysis from the Manus AI Product Blueprint, these are the four specific moments that cause a developer to abandon their current tool stack and commit permanently to a new one. DevPulse must create all four of these moments in the first week of use.

A developer imports their Postman collection. DevPulse finds 4 exposed API keys they did not know about. The developer thinks: this has been sitting in a public workspace for months and I had no idea. That shock is the most powerful acquisition moment possible. Design the Postman import UI to surface the credential finding immediately and specifically. Show the exact key, exact collection location, and exact service it exposes. This moment will be shared on Twitter and Reddit by every developer it happens to.

A developer opens DevPulse for the first time and sees that their slash summarize endpoint accounts for 73 percent of their total monthly LLM bill. They have been optimizing the wrong feature for three months. That moment of why did nobody tell me this creates instant loyalty. The UI must deliver this revelation as the very first thing visible in the first session. Not after setup. Not after configuration. Immediately on first open.

A pull request is flagged by DevPulse with a critical vulnerability before it merges. The developer fixes it in 20 minutes instead of discovering it in production. They tell their team lead. The team lead says install this on all our repos. This is how DevPulse spreads virally within organisations. One developer installs it, CI/CD catches something real, the whole team adopts it. The PR comment must be clean, specific, and contain actionable one-line remediation guidance.
TRIGGER 4 THE AUTONOMOUS KILL SWITCH MOMENT

As identified in the YC Hackathon document: a rogue AI agent script goes into an infinite reasoning loop making rapid LLM API calls. DevPulse detects the anomaly through its AgentGuard rate limiter and automatically severs the API key connection before the budget is blown. The developer gets a Slack alert: Your agent was spending 4,000 rupees per minute. DevPulse stopped it automatically and saved your budget. This visceral save-the-day moment is the demo that investors immediately understand and remember.
Section 3: What Is DevPulse — Complete Product Definition

DevPulse is not just an API scanner or a cost tracker. As identified by the Manus AI Product Blueprint, DevPulse is the Nervous System for AI-native companies. In a world where AI generates 41 percent of code and LLM costs are the fastest-growing operational expense, DevPulse is the only platform that ensures an application is simultaneously Secure, Profitable, and Compliant in a single unified workflow.
Engine 1 is the API Security Scanner.
OWASP Top 10 vulnerability scanning on every endpoint automatically.
Runs on every git commit through CI/CD integration.
API key and credential exposure detection before cloud sync.
Shadow API endpoint discovery from source code analysis.
One-click PCI DSS v4.0 and GDPR compliance report generation.
Import from Postman, Bruno, and OpenAPI Specification formats.
AI-powered inline fix suggestions delivered inside VS Code.
Engine 2 is the API Cost Intelligence System.
Real-time LLM token usage tracking per endpoint.
Per-feature and per-user cost attribution at granular level.
OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, and Cohere supported.
Budget alerts that fire before overspending occurs.
Thinking token cost isolation using differential computation.
Smart model routing recommendations per task type.
Autonomous Kill Switch that stops rogue agent loops automatically.
The AgentGuard Layer — Unique to DevPulse

As identified in the YC Hackathon document, DevPulse has a capability no competitor has even attempted: AgentGuard. This is the autonomous protection layer that sits between AI agent code and LLM providers. It detects when an agent enters an infinite reasoning loop, when cost anomalies exceed configured thresholds, and when sensitive data like PII is being sent to an LLM without redaction. It then takes autonomous action by pausing the agent, alerting the developer, and preventing budget damage. This is the future of agentic AI safety and DevPulse owns it at the developer tier.
VS Code as the Core Distribution Strategy

The VS Code extension is not a feature. It is the survival requirement. As confirmed by the Manus AI audit and the YC Hackathon document independently, the VS Code extension is the most critical component to build because it is the distribution channel. A developer who installs DevPulse as a VS Code extension becomes a daily user. A developer who accesses DevPulse through a browser dashboard uses it occasionally. All viral growth, all retention, and all workflow moat depends on VS Code integration.
73% of professional developers use VS Code daily
Verified by Stack Overflow 2025 Developer Survey
Addresses largest possible developer audience without any geographic or platform limitation
IDE security plugins growing at 21.1% CAGR to 2030
Fastest delivery channel in developer tooling space
Natural market tailwind that grows DevPulse distribution automatically
Developer never leaves their editor to use DevPulse
Highest adoption rate of any delivery channel. Tool used daily not occasionally.
First value in 60 seconds of installation
Extension shows cost data immediately using local workspace or API proxy
Satisfies the Zero Configuration Rule identified by Manus AI and YC document
Once in GitHub Actions pipeline, removing requires active justification
Retention mechanism that is almost never removed once installed
Section 4: Market Research and Verified Data
All market figures below are sourced from verified industry reports published in 2025 and 2026 and cross-referenced across multiple independent documents in this compilation.
Market Size and Growth Rates — 2026 Verified

The Founder's Destiny Blueprint cross-referenced document (Document 2 in this compilation) cited the following verified sources: Fortune Business Insights confirmed the API security market is projected to reach USD 1195.19 million in 2026 growing rapidly. Kong Inc reported in October 2025 that the rapidly changing API landscape is being transformed by AI agents becoming central to the ecosystem. Nordic APIs published in December 2025 that AI-driven API economy predictions for 2026 include new security demands and automation opportunities. CybelAngel published in December 2025 that API security risks and trends for 2026 include unmonitored legacy APIs and shadow APIs as primary data breach vectors.

The Herald News published on March 1, 2026 specifically identified the 2026 AI Cost Crisis, describing the rise of API aggregation platforms and their potential to deliver 80 percent cost savings. GCG published in January 2026 that the AI cost problem was the number one budget surprise that nobody prepared for in 2026. These publications confirm that the two core problems DevPulse solves are front-page news in 2026.
All developer security and LLM cost intelligence tools globally
Combined API security, application security, and AI cost monitoring
SMEs and AI startups using API-based architecture globally
Fastest growing segment at 17.5% CAGR. Primary DevPulse target.
India and Southeast Asia AI startup and SME, 3-year realistic capture
Conservative 1% of SAM over 36 months with current plan and budget

India has 5.8 million software developers, the second largest developer population globally, growing at 9 percent annually. Bangalore alone houses 40 percent of the country's tech talent. DevPulse launches in the highest-density concentration of target users on earth. As confirmed by the YC Hackathon document: we are building in Bangalore, the highest-density concentration of target users on earth. We operate at 15 percent of the cost of a Silicon Valley competitor while selling to a global market.

The precedent is proven. Postman was founded in Bangalore and became the global standard for API testing. Freshworks reached NASDAQ listing from Chennai. DevPulse follows this exact playbook: build an exceptional product in India, achieve product-market fit with Indian developers and AI startups, then expand globally through VS Code Marketplace and Product Hunt at zero geographic cost.

DevPulse competes in three overlapping market segments simultaneously. As confirmed by both Claude analysis and the Manus AI technical audit, no existing product unifies all three. This is the structural market gap that defines DevPulse's category position.
30K+ workspace leaks. Free tier killed March 2026. 1GB+ RAM usage. Zero security scanning. Zero cost intelligence. Primary migration target.
Git-native and good. But zero security, zero cost intelligence, zero AgentGuard.
Lives in VS Code correctly. But zero security, zero cost, zero CI/CD, zero agent protection.
Post-Kong acquisition cloud sync issues. Limited security. Zero cost intelligence.
Privacy-first web-based tool. No security engine, no cost attribution.
Design plus test plus mock. Steep learning curve. Zero security or cost intelligence.
Enterprise only. No cost intel. No VS Code. Manus AI correctly notes they miss Shadow APIs created by AI agents on the fly.
Enterprise post-acquisition pricing. No cost intel. No developer tier.
No cost intelligence. No VS Code native. No shadow API at this price.
Narrow OpenAPI spec auditing only. No cost intel. No IDE plugin.
Good open-source option. No cost intelligence. No automated compliance.
Single purpose security. No cost. No VS Code. No agent protection.
Full stack tracing but enterprise only. No LLM cost intelligence.
No security scanning. No VS Code. No thinking token isolation. No AgentGuard.
No security. Self-hosted complexity. No thinking token attribution.
Fully free but zero security. Self-hosted only. No IDE integration. No agent kill switch.
Open-source and free. No security capability. No per-feature attribution.
SDK not IDE extension. No security. Manual tagging only. No agent protection.
Enterprise at $500+/month. Complete overkill. No agent-specific features.
The Full Competitive Matrix — DevPulse Is the Only Green Row
Section 6: The 7 Structural Market Gaps DevPulse Owns
These gaps are confirmed by all seven source documents independently. They are structural absences in the market, not feature improvements. DevPulse owns all seven.

No Single Tool Combines API Security and LLM Cost Intelligence. Security tools do not care about your OpenAI bill. Cost tools do not care about your BOLA vulnerability. Zero products combine both in one workflow. A global patent search confirmed zero prior art for this combination. This is a category-defining invention.

Thinking Token Cost Attribution Using Differential Computation and Timing Signals. No tool isolates thinking tokens from standard output tokens, attributes them to specific application features, uses response timing as secondary detection, or generates prompt optimisation recommendations. Global patent database search returned zero prior art worldwide.

Autonomous AI Agent Cost Protection with Kill Switch. No tool automatically detects infinite LLM reasoning loops, applies configurable budget thresholds per agent, and severs API connections before catastrophic cost damage occurs. The AgentGuard capability in DevPulse is entirely novel and addresses the fastest-growing developer anxiety in 2026.
GAP 4 IDE FIRST SHADOW API DISCOVERY

Shadow API Discovery Without Production Infrastructure. Enterprise shadow API tools cost $2,000 per month minimum and require production network deployment. No tool performs discovery through IDE-level static route extraction from source code correlated with local development traffic. DevPulse delivers this at under 100 USD per month.

Automated PCI DSS v4.0 Compliance Evidence from Continuous DAST Output. PCI DSS version 4.0.1 enacted March 2025 mandates continuous API security testing and documented evidence. Existing compliance tools cost 15,000 to 100,000 USD for QSA engagement. DevPulse delivers this at 99 USD per month, unlocking the entire fintech and healthtech segment.

Postman Collection Import as Top-of-Funnel Conversion Mechanism. Five hundred thousand companies are actively evaluating alternatives right now. No competitor built a one-click import that scans a Postman collection, detects credentials, and shows cost exposure in under 60 seconds. The Manus AI Product Blueprint called this the key to the kingdom.

The Single Tool for API Testing, Security, Cost, and Agent Protection in VS Code. Developers currently manage four or more separate tools with zero integration between them. DevPulse collapses all of it into one VS Code extension. Nobody has made this unified experience their core pitch and the positioning is completely unclaimed in the global market.
Section 7: Technical Build Status and What to Build Next

The Manus AI technical audit of the DevPulse repository provides the most accurate picture of current build status. This section combines that audit with the YC Hackathon development roadmap and the Claude build analysis.
What Is Already Built — Infrastructure Layer Complete
backend/services/scanner.py checks HTTPS, CORS, and security headers
Foundation for OWASP expansion in Phase 1
backend/routers/llm.py logs tokens, model names, and INR cost
Foundation for thinking token attribution in Phase 2
Core of the Kill Switch demo for YC hackathon
Trust foundation that makes DevPulse safe for enterprise keys
Distribution channel scaffold ready to extend
Frontend dashboard modern and well-structured per Manus AI audit
Robust and scalable infrastructure confirmed by Manus AI
Solid data foundation for all intelligence features
The Six Missing Intelligence Features — Ordered by Business Impact

As confirmed by both the Claude analysis and the Manus AI audit independently, these six features are the difference between a prototype and a patent-grade market-ready product. Each one is buildable in one to three days of focused work.

Build a Python parser for Postman Collection v2.1 JSON format that recursively traverses the item tree and extracts all endpoint definitions. Then immediately trigger an OWASP security scan and credential detection scan on each extracted endpoint. The YC Hackathon document calls this the Postman Refugee Engine. The Manus AI document calls it the key to the kingdom. The Claude analysis identified it as the primary acquisition engine. All three documents agree: build this first. One focused day of Python work.
BUILD SECOND UNIFIED RISK SCORE ENGINE

Write the Python function that takes security severity and cost anomaly data for the same endpoint identifier and produces one combined score. This is the core of Patent 1. The formula: combined score equals security weight times vulnerability severity plus cost weight times cost anomaly ratio. This is the moment in every demo where a developer understands why DevPulse exists. One afternoon of focused Python work.

As specifically described in the YC Hackathon document: modify the api-proxy edge function to intercept responses from OpenAI o1 and o3 and Anthropic Claude models. Parse the usage metadata to separate reasoning tokens from completion tokens. Add a timer measuring wall-clock latency between request and first response byte as the secondary signal. Store measurements attributed to the originating endpoint. This is Patent 2 and the first-in-world technical claim. Thirty to forty lines added to the existing proxy. One day of focused work.
BUILD FOURTH AUTONOMOUS KILL SWITCH DEMO

As described in the YC Hackathon document for Day 2 of the hackathon: build a script that intentionally enters an infinite reasoning loop making rapid API calls. Show the DevPulse AgentGuard dashboard detecting the anomaly through the existing rate-limiter edge function and automatically severing the API connection before the budget is blown. This is the visceral demo moment that investors immediately understand. The infrastructure already exists. This is connecting the existing components and building the demo flow.
BUILD FIFTH PCI DSS COMPLIANCE MAPPING

Create the Python dictionary mapping each OWASP API Security Top 10 category to specific PCI DSS v4.0.1 requirement identifiers and GDPR article references. Then build the PDF report generator on top. This unlocks the entire fintech and healthtech customer segment immediately. Two hours for the mapping dictionary, one day for the report generator.
BUILD SIXTH SHADOW API WORKSPACE SCANNER

Add a TypeScript function to the VS Code extension that scans all JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python files in the open workspace for framework-specific route definition patterns across Express, FastAPI, Django REST, Spring Boot, and Laravel. Compare against the backend route inventory and surface shadow endpoints in the VS Code sidebar. Most complex of the six. Two to three days of TypeScript work.

The YC Hackathon document provides a specific three-day execution plan designed to produce a demo that wins the YC India hackathon. This plan is directly applicable to the DevPulse build sequence regardless of whether a hackathon is the immediate context.
Connect existing Supabase backend to a basic VS Code extension
VS Code sidebar that authenticates and displays the user's current agent budget
Proves developer workflow integration. Shows you understand shift-left.
Implement thinking token proxy logic and autonomous Kill Switch trigger
api-proxy intercepting and categorizing reasoning tokens. AgentGuard firing autonomously.
Proves technical depth. Validates patent claims. Shows next-generation AI thinking.
Polish the Disaster Demo and finalize the pitch deck
A seamless 3-minute live demo showing problem, VS Code integration, and autonomous resolution
The visceral save-the-day moment that investors immediately understand and remember.
Section 8: Patent Portfolio and Global Overlap Analysis

DevPulse has four provisional patent applications ready for submission through New Horizon College of Engineering to the Indian Patent Office, Bengaluru Branch. All four confirmed with zero global prior art on core claims.

Unified API Security and LLM Cost Intelligence
A Method and System for Unified Real-Time API Security Vulnerability Detection and Large Language Model API Cost Intelligence Within IDEs. The foundational DevPulse invention. No product or patent anywhere combines both domains in a single unified system with a combined risk score.

Thinking Token Cost Attribution
A Method for Detection, Attribution, and Anomaly Classification of Thinking Token Expenditure in LLM API Calls Using Differential Token Analysis and Response Timing Signatures. Zero prior art found globally. Thinking tokens existed only since OpenAI o1 in September 2024. Most commercially valuable patent in portfolio.
NHCE/DEV/2026/003 ZERO PRIOR ART FOR METHOD

Shadow API Discovery via IDE Correlation
A Method for Shadow API Endpoint Discovery Through IDE Framework-Specific Static Route Extraction and Local Development Traffic Correlation. Novel approach operating at IDE level without production infrastructure. All enterprise competitors require $2,000+ per month and production network deployment.
NHCE/DEV/2026/004 ZERO AT SME PRICE POINT

Automated PCI DSS Compliance Evidence Generation
A Method and System for Automated Generation of API Security Compliance Evidence Reports for PCI DSS v4.0.1 and GDPR from Continuous DAST Output. PCI DSS v4.0.1 is one year old. Zero affordable tools generate this evidence automatically. Unlocks fintech and healthtech segments immediately.
Unified API Security and LLM Cost Intelligence NHCE/DEV/2026/001
Combined intelligence document for patent attorney background reference
Application for Grant of Patent, one per invention
Provisional Specification, content provided in each patent PDF
Declaration as to Inventorship signed by K S Akshay
File international PCT application within 12 months covering 158 countries
Section 9: Funding Strategy and Target Investors

This section combines the Claude funding analysis, the Manus AI funding roadmap, the Founder's Destiny Blueprint investor research, and the YC Hackathon positioning strategy into one complete funding plan.
The Astrological and Market Timing Alignment
JUNE TO JULY 2026 IS THE OPTIMAL FUNDING WINDOW

The Founder's Destiny Blueprint identified the Jupiter transit moving into Cancer in June 2026, entering the fifth house of innovation and new ventures for a Pisces Ascendant, as a Golden Period for raising funding and going live. Saturn's transit through Pisces in the first house demands disciplined execution and rewards founders who build lasting foundations. This astrological window aligns precisely with the real market window: peak Postman refugee momentum, PCI DSS v4.0.1 enforcement becoming serious, and the 2026 AI cost crisis becoming front-page developer news. Begin serious investor conversations in April 2026 for a June to July 2026 funding close.
Priority Funding Targets — From Founder's Blueprint Research
Why They Are Right for DevPulse
Specifically launched AI Cohort 2026 partnered with Google Labs AI Futures Fund. Backed Postman through Nexus category. Top SaaS investor in Bengaluru.
Apply immediately. Next cohort after March 2026 batch of 5 startups.
Seed platform for company builders. Accepts applications year-round. Strong SaaS and developer tools track record.
Apply now. Do not wait for cohort timing.
YC India hackathon is the direct entry point. YC has deep interest in developer tools and AI security. Manus AI identified YC as highly relevant.
YC India Hackathon then YC application
Win the hackathon. Use that as the YC application story.
Operates as startup accelerator and early-stage seed fund. Backs enterprise SaaS, fintech, and healthcare. All three are DevPulse segments.
Apply in April 2026 to align with June funding window.
Government initiative offering up to 40% grants for AI startups. Non-dilutive capital. Identified by Manus AI as specific opportunity.
Apply for non-dilutive grant capital to extend runway before VC round.
Google for Startups and AWS Activate
Cloud provider credits up to $100,000 each. Manus AI specifically identified these as infrastructure cost offset.
Apply immediately. Zero dilution. Offsets entire infrastructure cost for 12 months.
Funding Ask and Use of Funds

Section 10: Founder's Numerological and Astrological Alignment

From the Founder's Destiny Blueprint (2026): "The Alignment of Vision, Strategy, and Universal Timing." Founder: K S Akshay. Date of Birth: August 31, 2004. Time of Birth: 8:28 PM. Place of Birth: Bellary, Karnataka.
Sees what others miss. Shadow APIs, hidden costs, invisible thinking tokens. These are Rahu themes made into product features.
Warrior spirit, courage, drive for societal good. Securing the internet aligns with Number 9 mission. Mars gives technical execution energy.
Master alignment. Psychic Number 4 and Business Name Number 4 indicate structural reliability and foundational strength. Built to last.
Name and Destiny in perfect alignment. The person and the mission are one. Strongest possible founder-product alignment in numerology.
Company named after parents carries ancestral blessing and permanence. This is not a side project name. It is a declaration.

From the Founder's Destiny Blueprint: The Pisces Ascendant with Jupiter as Ascendant Lord gives a philosophical, visionary mindset combined with the ability to identify market gaps others cannot see. Moon in Capricorn gives discipline, long-term thinking, and the ability to build lasting structures under pressure. Sun in Virgo gives precise, analytical technical skills and attention to detail, essential for security and cost intelligence work. Mars in the chart gives the warrior spirit needed to challenge established players like Postman and Salt Security.
Golden Period for funding and launch. Fifth house of innovation activated for Pisces Ascendant.
Target public launch and primary funding pitch for June to July 2026. This is the universe-aligned window.
First house of self and foundation. Demands disciplined hard work. Rewards builders who focus.
Do not scatter effort. Build the six missing features in exact priority order. Discipline creates the legacy Saturn promises.
Amplifies ability to see hidden things. Shadow APIs and hidden costs are Rahu themes.
The product literally embodies the Rahu energy. Every feature DevPulse builds is a manifestation of the founder's Rahu-ruled Psychic Number 4.
Energy for execution, launches, and competitive moves.
Use March and April for maximum build velocity. The energy supports rapid technical execution in this window.
THE STRATEGIC VERDICT FROM THE DESTINY BLUEPRINT

You will succeed because: Rahu gives you the ability to identify and monetise invisible market gaps that others cannot see. Mars gives you the warrior spirit to compete against well-funded incumbents. Jupiter in 2026 gives you the expansion energy to attract investors and partners. Saturn demands the disciplined execution that builds lasting companies. The combination of Number 4 Psychic and Number 4 Business Name is a master structural alignment indicating DevPulse is built to endure. The universe is aligned. The market is ready. Execute.
Section 11: Revenue Model and Financial Projections
50 API scans per month, basic cost dashboard, 1 API, VS Code extension, limited AgentGuard
Unlimited scans, 3 APIs, full LLM cost tracking, secret scanner, email alerts, basic AgentGuard
AI startups and teams of 2 to 10
CI/CD integration, 10 APIs, budget alerts, Slack, shadow API 3 endpoints, PCI DSS reports, full AgentGuard
Unlimited APIs, team dashboard, full compliance reports, unlimited shadow API, SSO, enterprise AgentGuard
Custom SLA, white-label reports, dedicated CSM, custom compliance, compliance API access
Section 12: Exit Strategy and Acquirer Map

As identified in the Manus AI Product Blueprint: to be acquired by a giant like Meta, Microsoft, or Palo Alto Networks, you must own the developer workflow. DevPulse achieves this through VS Code integration, CI/CD pipeline embedding, and the AgentGuard layer that becomes essential infrastructure.
Extend API security to SME developer segment. Add LLM cost intelligence layer.
Owns Insomnia. Security and cost intelligence is natural product extension.
Developer security leader. API security and cost is adjacent expansion.
Full observability platform. API security plus LLM cost fits perfectly.
Mentioned by Manus AI Product Blueprint as acquisition target. Security platform strategy.
VS Code extension with large install base is natural for GitHub/Microsoft strategy.

Developer security SaaS tools trade at 10 to 20 times ARR at strategic acquisition. At $1M ARR, achievable by Month 30 to 36, DevPulse is worth $10 to $20 million to a strategic acquirer. At $5M ARR the range becomes $50 to $100 million. Pre-seed investors receiving 5 percent equity for a 15 lakh investment receive 10 to 15 million USD at the $1M ARR exit. That is 67 to 100 times return on invested capital at the 3-year milestone.

This document combines all seven source documents into one definitive master reference. Every key fact, every verified data point, every strategic recommendation from every document is consolidated here.
Nervous System for AI-native companies. Secure plus Profitable plus Compliant in one VS Code workflow.
$1.8B API security market growing at 33.2% CAGR to $5.69B by 2030.
$15B plus growing to $45B plus by 2028 at 44% plus CAGR.
3.5 hours per week wasted on tool management. 73% use VS Code daily. 41% AI-generated code.
87% distrust AI tool accuracy. 66% frustrated by AI that misses the mark. DevPulse solves this with proof not promises.
500,000 companies switching now. 30,000+ workspace credential leaks. 40% valuation drop.
Zero products globally combine security scanning and LLM cost intelligence. This is a category gap not a feature gap.
YC Hackathon doc and Claude analysis
No competitor offers autonomous AI agent cost protection with kill switch. First-in-world capability.
Unified security and cost co-analysis. Zero global prior art. 7 claims.
Thinking token attribution. First in world. Most commercially valuable. 6 claims.
Shadow API discovery via IDE correlation. Zero prior art for method. 6 claims.
PCI DSS compliance evidence from DAST. Zero prior art at SME price. 6 claims.

Unified Risk Score Engine. Core of Patent 1. One afternoon of Python work.
